Original_FR: "if the item the user wants is available for exchange, the user can request the exchange of items that he/she has with the item he/she wants."
Conflicting_FR: Users can request exchanges only if the items are unavailable.
Resolution:
1. Revise Requirement B to allow exchanges for both available and unavailable items, aligning with Requirement A's broader scope: "Users can request exchanges regardless of the availability of the items they want."
2. Modify Requirement A to limit exchanges to unavailable items, aligning with Requirement B's restriction: "Users can request the exchange of items they have only if the item they want is unavailable."
3. Introduce a hybrid rule to accommodate both scenarios: "Users can request exchanges for unavailable items by default, but exchanges for available items may require additional conditions, such as priority access or approval."

Original_FR: "if the user no longer wishes to sell/exchange the item, he/she can remove the item from the system by touching a??Remove the itema?? button."
Conflicting_FR: Items shall remain in the system even if the user clicks "Remove the item".
Resolution:
1. Modify Requirement A to specify that the "Remove the item" button archives or hides the item from public view instead of completely deleting it from the system.  
2. Update Requirement B to clarify that items remain in the system but are flagged as inactive or hidden when the user clicks "Remove the item."  
3. Introduce a new requirement stating that users can permanently delete items only through an additional confirmation step, ensuring consistency with both requirements.

Original_FR: "if the user no longer wishes to use the system, he/she can click a??Delete Profilea?? button. After deleting the profile, the user will no longer be able to use the system with the account details that he entered before."
Conflicting_FR: The system shall retain user account and allow login even after clicking "Delete Profile".
Resolution:
1. Redefine the "Delete Profile" functionality to differentiate between a "soft delete" (disabling the account temporarily but retaining data for reactivation) and a "hard delete" (permanent deletion of account details), and allow the user to select their preferred option.  
2. Update Requirement B to specify that the system shall retain user account data for a defined grace period (e.g., 30 days) after clicking "Delete Profile," during which the user can reactivate their account, after which the data will be permanently deleted.  
3. Introduce a confirmation step for "Delete Profile" where users explicitly choose whether they want the account to be permanently deleted or only deactivated, aligning the system behavior with their choice.

Original_FR: 10000 users should be able to use the system at the same time.
Conflicting_FR: The system shall restrict the number of concurrent users to a maximum of 5000 to maintain response time and data consistency.
Resolution:
1. Conduct a performance optimization analysis to determine if the system can be upgraded to support 10,000 concurrent users while maintaining response time and data consistency, and adjust Requirement B accordingly.  
2. Revise Requirement A to specify that up to 10,000 users can access the system, but only 5,000 can be active concurrently, ensuring alignment with Requirement B.  
3. Implement a tiered user access system where up to 10,000 users can log in, but only 5,000 are allowed concurrent access, with a queuing mechanism to manage additional users.

Original_FR: 10000 users should be able to use the system at the same time.
Conflicting_FR: The system shall allow administrative concurrent access only once per system startup and revoke all administrative sessions automatically within 5 seconds after they begin.
Resolution:
1. Modify Requirement B to allow administrative sessions to coexist with user sessions by extending the administrative session duration or enabling multiple concurrent administrative accesses while maintaining security controls.  
2. Adjust Requirement A to specify that administrative users are excluded from the 10,000 concurrent user limit, ensuring administrative access does not interfere with regular user capacity.  
3. Introduce a priority mechanism where administrative access temporarily overrides regular user access during critical operations, while ensuring minimal disruption to the 10,000 user capacity.

Original_FR: A CEP card may contain currencies that the load acquirer does not support. As a result, load devices must use the alphabetic issuer-supplied currency code and currency exponent from the CEP slot(s) to display the source amounts for the currency conversion transaction.
Conflicting_FR: Load devices shall reject any CEP card containing currencies not supported by the load acquirer, and no source amount shall be displayed.
Resolution:
1. Define a priority rule: Specify whether displaying the source amounts (Requirement A) or rejecting unsupported currencies outright (Requirement B) takes precedence, based on business goals or regulatory requirements.  
2. Introduce a conditional process: Allow load devices to display source amounts for unsupported currencies (Requirement A) only in a diagnostic or informational mode, while still rejecting the transaction as per Requirement B.  
3. Modify the system behavior: Require load devices to validate supported currencies before attempting to display any source amount, ensuring that unsupported currencies are rejected without any display.

Original_FR: A class shall be either a non-clinical class or a clinical class.
Conflicting_FR: A class may contain both clinical and non-clinical components within the same structure.
Resolution:
1. Revise Requirement A to state: "A class may be primarily classified as either non-clinical or clinical, but it can contain components of both types within the same structure."  
2. Split the concept of "class" into two distinct entities: "Class Type" (clinical or non-clinical) and "Class Components" (which may include both clinical and non-clinical elements).  
3. Introduce a hierarchical structure where a class is categorized as clinical or non-clinical at the top level, but subcomponents within the class can include both types to accommodate mixed content.

Original_FR: A CND as defined in clause 3 may use the CNG to access NGN services.
Conflicting_FR: A CND shall not be allowed to access NGN services through the CNG interface for security reasons.
Resolution:
1. Update Requirement A to specify that a CND may use the CNG to access NGN services only if security measures are implemented to mitigate risks outlined in Requirement B.  
2. Modify Requirement B to allow conditional access to NGN services through the CNG interface, contingent on meeting predefined security protocols and safeguards.  
3. Introduce a new requirement that mandates a security assessment and approval process for CND access to NGN services via the CNG interface, ensuring alignment between both requirements.

Original_FR: A cohort shall be associated to one Program of Study offered by the department.
Conflicting_FR: A cohort may be associated with multiple Programs of Study across departments.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the business rules by determining whether a cohort must be limited to one Program of Study for administrative simplicity or if flexibility across multiple Programs of Study is essential for operational needs. Update the requirements accordingly.  
2. Introduce a hierarchical relationship where a cohort is primarily associated with one primary Program of Study but can optionally have secondary associations with additional Programs of Study across departments.  
3. Define different types of cohorts (e.g., "single-program cohorts" and "multi-program cohorts") to accommodate both scenarios, ensuring clear rules for each type.

Original_FR: A cohort shall be associated to one Program of Study offered by the department.
Conflicting_FR: A cohort's Program of Study may be changed only once per calendar year and any change must be applied within a 10-second transaction window; changes attempted outside this window shall be rejected even if performed by DEPARTMENT_ADMIN.
Resolution:
1. Clarify whether a cohort is strictly tied to a single Program of Study for its entire lifecycle (as per Requirement A) or if changes are permissible (as per Requirement B), and update the requirements to reflect the intended policy.
2. Modify Requirement A to explicitly allow for the possibility of changing a cohort's Program of Study under the conditions specified in Requirement B, ensuring consistency between the two requirements.
3. If changes to a cohort's Program of Study are not allowed, remove Requirement B or revise it to apply to individual students rather than cohorts, aligning with the intent of Requirement A.

Original_FR: A CPN user shall be able to configure any unmanaged service provided to the user.
Conflicting_FR: A CPN user shall not be allowed to configure unmanaged services without administrator approval.
Resolution:
1. Modify Requirement A to specify that a CPN user can configure unmanaged services only with administrator approval, ensuring alignment with Requirement B.  
2. Update Requirement B to allow CPN users to configure unmanaged services but notify administrators for approval post-configuration.  
3. Introduce a tiered user role system where certain users (e.g., power users) can configure unmanaged services autonomously, while others require administrator approval.

Original_FR: A current temperature value that is received from an individual thermostat shall be compared to the valid temperature range values.
Conflicting_FR: The system shall not perform validation against predefined range values for temperature inputs.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the system's purpose and determine whether temperature validation is a mandatory feature for safety or functionality. If validation is critical, update Requirement B to allow predefined range checks.  
2. Introduce a configuration option where validation against predefined ranges is optional and can be enabled or disabled based on system needs, satisfying both requirements.  
3. Define specific contexts or scenarios where validation is required (e.g., safety-critical operations) and others where it is not, refining both requirements to coexist without conflict.

Original_FR: A description and time/date of each prescription that has been ordered or given is displayed starting with most recent.
Conflicting_FR: The system shall display prescriptions in chronological order from oldest to newest.
Resolution:
1. Introduce a user-configurable option allowing users to toggle between displaying prescriptions from most recent to oldest or from oldest to most recent, thus accommodating both requirements.
2. Define specific contexts or user roles where one ordering method is preferred over the other, such as displaying most recent first for healthcare providers and oldest first for administrative reviews.
3. Prioritize one requirement based on stakeholder input or system usage analysis, and adjust the other requirement to align with the chosen priority ordering method.

Original_FR: A failed login will alert the physician/pharmacist.
Conflicting_FR: A failed login attempt shall not generate alerts to users for security reasons.
Resolution:
1. Define a tiered alert system where failed login attempts are logged and reviewed by an administrator, but real-time alerts are only sent to physicians/pharmacists if multiple suspicious failed attempts occur within a predefined timeframe.  
2. Modify Requirement A to specify that only critical failed login attempts (e.g., those involving multiple sensitive accounts) will alert the physician/pharmacist, while Requirement B remains applicable to routine failed attempts.  
3. Introduce a configurable setting allowing organizations to choose whether failed login alerts are sent to physicians/pharmacists or handled exclusively by security personnel, based on their security policies.

Original_FR: A failed login will alert the physician/pharmacist.
Conflicting_FR: Login failure alerts shall be delivered only once to Security Administrators and thereafter be suppressed for 24 hours, regardless of subsequent failures.
Resolution:
1. Define separate alert mechanisms: Allow failed login alerts to be sent to physicians/pharmacists for their accounts and to Security Administrators for broader monitoring, with suppression rules applying only to the Security Administrators' alerts.  
2. Introduce configurable alert settings: Enable system administrators to configure who receives alerts (e.g., physicians/pharmacists or Security Administrators) and specify suppression rules for each recipient group independently.  
3. Prioritize alert recipients: Specify that physicians/pharmacists receive immediate alerts for their own failed login attempts, while Security Administrators adhere to the 24-hour suppression rule for system-wide login failure alerts.

Original_FR: A flight pattern shall document the longitude latitude and altitude of waypoints in a flight route as offsets from the starting position of the UAV.
Conflicting_FR: A flight pattern shall document the magnetic bearing and distance of waypoints in a flight route as offsets from the starting position of the UAV.
Resolution:
1. Combine both requirements to allow the flight pattern to document waypoints using both formats: longitude/latitude/altitude and magnetic bearing/distance, enabling flexibility based on operational needs.  
2. Define a configurable option in the system to let users select their preferred format (longitude/latitude/altitude or magnetic bearing/distance) for documenting waypoints in the flight pattern.  
3. Standardize one format (e.g., longitude/latitude/altitude) as the primary method while providing a conversion tool to derive the other format (magnetic bearing/distance) for users requiring it.

Original_FR: A flight pattern shall document the longitude latitude and altitude of waypoints in a flight route as offsets from the starting position of the UAV.
Conflicting_FR: A flight pattern shall record waypoints using absolute geographic coordinates rather than offsets.
Resolution:
1. Update Requirement A to allow the option of documenting waypoints as either offsets or absolute geographic coordinates, based on mission needs, and specify the format clearly for each option.  
2. Combine both requirements into a single unified requirement that mandates recording waypoints in both formats—offsets for relative navigation and absolute geographic coordinates for global positioning.  
3. Conduct a stakeholder review to determine the preferred format (offsets or absolute geographic coordinates) and revise both requirements to align with the dominant use case.

Original_FR: A list of all dispute cases that meet the entered search criteria must be provided to the user. The list of cases must differentiate the type and/or status of the case by color-coding the line item in the list.
Conflicting_FR: The system shall provide a textual indication of case status instead of using color-coding.
Resolution:
1. Modify Requirement A to allow both color-coding and textual indication, giving users the option to choose their preferred method of differentiation in the system settings.  
2. Replace color-coding in Requirement A with a standardized textual indication as specified in Requirement B, ensuring accessibility for all users.  
3. Implement both color-coding and textual indication simultaneously, ensuring that the system provides redundant cues to accommodate different user needs and preferences.

Original_FR: A list of all dispute cases that meet the entered search criteria must be provided to the user. The list of cases must differentiate the type and/or status of the case by color-coding the line item in the list.
Conflicting_FR: The list of dispute cases shall differentiate type/status using textual tags instead of color-coding.
Resolution:
1. Combine both approaches by using both color-coding and textual tags, allowing users to benefit from visual differentiation while ensuring accessibility for those unable to perceive colors.  
2. Replace color-coding entirely with textual tags to ensure compliance with accessibility standards, prioritizing Requirement B.  
3. Make the differentiation method configurable, allowing users to choose between color-coding, textual tags, or both based on their preference.

Original_FR: A list of available patients is displayed after login.
Conflicting_FR: The list of available patients shall be accessible only after explicit user request, not automatically after login.
Resolution:
1. Modify Requirement A to specify that the list of available patients is displayed only after the user explicitly requests it post-login, aligning it with Requirement B.  
2. Adjust Requirement B to allow an optional configuration setting where users can choose whether the patient list is displayed automatically after login or only upon explicit request.  
3. Introduce a compromise where a notification or prompt after login informs users about the availability of the patient list, requiring them to confirm or request access explicitly.

Original_FR: A meeting scheduler system will inform the ???potential meeting participant???? of the meeting.
Conflicting_FR: The meeting scheduler shall notify only confirmed attendees, excluding tentative participants.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the intended scope of notifications by defining whether "potential meeting participants" in Requirement A includes tentative participants and align this with Requirement B's exclusion criteria.
2. Update Requirement A to explicitly state that notifications are sent only to confirmed attendees, ensuring consistency with Requirement B.
3. Introduce a configurable notification option in the system, allowing users to choose whether to notify tentative participants or only confirmed attendees, accommodating both requirements.

Original_FR: A meeting scheduler system will inform the potential meeting participant of the meeting.
Conflicting_FR: The meeting scheduler system shall not automatically notify participants until the meeting is confirmed.
Resolution:
1. Modify Requirement A to specify that the system will only inform participants of the meeting after it has been confirmed, ensuring alignment with Requirement B.  
2. Update Requirement B to allow limited notifications (e.g., tentative meeting invitations) while clarifying that final confirmation notifications will only be sent after the meeting is confirmed.  
3. Introduce a new requirement to distinguish between tentative and confirmed notifications, ensuring that Requirement A applies to tentative notifications and Requirement B governs confirmed notifications.

Original_FR: A meeting scheduler system will temporarily reserve the ???time slots???? for the proposed meeting.
Conflicting_FR: The scheduler shall not reserve time slots until the meeting is finalized.
Resolution:
1. Introduce a "tentative reservation" feature where time slots are marked as tentative but not fully reserved until the meeting is finalized, ensuring clarity for users and alignment with both requirements.  
2. Modify Requirement A to specify that time slots are only "soft-held" (visible but not blocked) to avoid conflicts with Requirement B's restriction on reserving slots before finalization.  
3. Adjust Requirement B to allow temporary reservations with a clear expiration time, ensuring unused tentative slots are released if the meeting is not finalized within a defined period.

Original_FR: A MODIS Computer Software Configuration Item (CSCI) is any independently tracked and configured unit of software within the MODIS SDP S/W, including processes, scripts, and utility libraries.  Each CSCI in the MODIS SDP S/W shall run in the ECS production environment.
Conflicting_FR: Access to MODIS CSCI configuration data shall be permitted to MODIS_SYSTEM_ADMIN holders only during a predefined maintenance window of 2 minutes per week; outside that 2-minute window no access or modification is allowed.
Resolution:
1. Modify Requirement A to specify that MODIS CSCIs must operate continuously in the ECS production environment, except during the predefined 2-minute maintenance window outlined in Requirement B.  
2. Extend Requirement B to allow read-only access to MODIS CSCI configuration data outside the maintenance window, while restricting modifications to the 2-minute window.  
3. Introduce a new requirement specifying that critical MODIS CSCIs must have redundancy or failover mechanisms to ensure uninterrupted operation during the maintenance window.

Original_FR: A MODIS Computer Software Configuration Item (CSCI) is any independently tracked and configured unit of software within the MODIS SDP S/W, including processes, scripts, and utility libraries.  Each CSCI within the MODIS SDP S/W systemshall employ the Earth Science Data and Information System (ESDIS)-supplied SDPTK mandatory functions to access the DAAC production environment and services.
Conflicting_FR: Use of ESDIS SDPTK functions shall be rate-limited to one invocation per 24 hours per DAAC administrator; any additional attempts within the period are rejected even if initiated by DAAC admins.
Resolution:
1. Update Requirement A to specify that the employment of ESDIS SDPTK mandatory functions must comply with rate-limiting constraints outlined in Requirement B, ensuring compatibility between software design and operational restrictions.  
2. Modify Requirement B to allow exceptions for critical or automated processes initiated by MODIS CSCI components, with appropriate logging and monitoring to prevent abuse.  
3. Introduce a mechanism in the MODIS SDP S/W system to queue and schedule SDPTK function invocations, ensuring adherence to the rate limit while maintaining system functionality.

Original_FR: A MODIS CSCI ... shall be coded according to the standards specified in SDST-022.
Conflicting_FR: Each CSCI may be coded according to team-specific internal guidelines if equivalent to SDST-022.
Resolution:
1. Update Requirement B to explicitly state that internal guidelines must be reviewed and approved to ensure equivalence with SDST-022 before use.  
2. Modify Requirement A to allow for exceptions where internal guidelines are demonstrated to meet or exceed the standards in SDST-022.  
3. Introduce a clarification document or process that defines the criteria and review mechanism for determining equivalence between internal guidelines and SDST-022.

Original_FR: A MODIS CSCI ... shall employ ESDIS-supplied SDPTK mandatory functions to access the DAAC environment.
Conflicting_FR: A CSCI may use custom-developed functions to access DAAC services instead of ESDIS-supplied SDPTK.
Resolution:
1. Update Requirement B to explicitly state that custom-developed functions may only be used if ESDIS-supplied SDPTK mandatory functions are proven insufficient or unavailable for specific use cases, ensuring alignment with Requirement A.
2. Introduce a priority rule stating that ESDIS-supplied SDPTK mandatory functions must be the default access method, with custom-developed functions allowed only as exceptions upon formal approval.
3. Combine both requirements into a single unified statement specifying that the CSCI must primarily use ESDIS-supplied SDPTK mandatory functions, with optional use of custom-developed functions under predefined conditions.

Original_FR: A MODIS CSCI ... shall run in the ECS production environment.
Conflicting_FR: Each CSCI shall run in a controlled test environment before deployment to ECS production.
Resolution:
1. Update Requirement A to explicitly state that the MODIS CSCI must first undergo testing in a controlled test environment before running in the ECS production environment, ensuring alignment with Requirement B.  
2. Clarify Requirement B to specify that testing in a controlled environment is a mandatory prerequisite for all deployments, including the MODIS CSCI, to avoid ambiguity.  
3. Introduce a new requirement that defines a standardized deployment process, explicitly requiring controlled test environment validation for all CSCIs before production deployment, ensuring consistency between both requirements.

Original_FR: A MySQL database will be used to store all information required to answer questions.
Conflicting_FR: A PostgreSQL database shall be used as the primary storage for all question-answer data.
Resolution:
1. Evaluate the technical and business advantages of MySQL versus PostgreSQL, then select one database as the standard and update both requirements accordingly.  
2. Define distinct roles for each database (e.g., use MySQL for backup or secondary storage and PostgreSQL as the primary database) and revise the requirements to reflect this division.  
3. Consult stakeholders to determine their preferences, constraints, and priorities, then align the requirements with the chosen database solution based on consensus.

Original_FR: A PGE ... Each PGE shall be provided with one or more sets of production rules specifying inputs, outputs, and activation conditions.
Conflicting_FR: A PGE shall execute dynamically without predefined production rules.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the scope of "production rules" in Requirement A to determine if they can be dynamically generated or adjusted during execution to align with Requirement B.  
2. Modify Requirement B to allow for predefined production rules as an initial configuration, while still enabling dynamic execution and adaptation during runtime.  
3. Introduce a hybrid approach where a PGE can optionally use predefined production rules from Requirement A but is designed to override or extend them dynamically, satisfying Requirement B.

Original_FR: A PGE ... shall not set or redefine environmental variables.
Conflicting_FR: A PGE shall be permitted to modify environmental variables during execution for configuration purposes.
Resolution:
1. Refine Requirement A to specify that a PGE shall not permanently set or redefine environmental variables but may temporarily modify them during execution if necessary for configuration purposes.  
2. Clarify Requirement B to explicitly state that modifications to environmental variables during execution must be temporary and reverted after the PGE completes execution.  
3. Introduce a new requirement to mandate that any changes to environmental variables by a PGE must be logged and documented to ensure transparency and traceability.

Original_FR: A PGE ... The SDST shall integrate all software processes into PGEs executing all processes in the MODIS TLCF.
Conflicting_FR: The SDST shall integrate only selected software processes into PGEs, not all processes.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the scope of software processes by defining specific criteria or categories to determine which processes must be integrated into PGEs, ensuring alignment between both requirements.  
2. Revise Requirement A or B to explicitly state whether "all processes" or "selected processes" are required, and provide a rationale for inclusion or exclusion to eliminate ambiguity.  
3. Conduct stakeholder discussions to prioritize processes based on operational importance or feasibility, and update the requirements accordingly to reflect a consensus.

Original_FR: A PGE ... The SDST shall integrate subsets of these processes into sets of PGEs that execute in DAAC centers.
Conflicting_FR: All PGEs shall execute within a unified ECS environment instead of separate DAAC centers.
Resolution:
1. Redefine the execution strategy to allow PGEs to operate within a unified ECS environment while maintaining logical subsets of processes that align with DAAC-specific needs, ensuring both requirements are partially satisfied.  
2. Implement a hybrid solution where PGEs execute in a unified ECS environment but retain DAAC-specific configurations and data access privileges to simulate DAAC-centered execution.  
3. Conduct stakeholder workshops to clarify the priority between centralized execution in ECS and DAAC-specific integration, then revise the requirements to align with the agreed-upon operational model.

Original_FR: A PGE is a script which executes one or more MODIS software processes within the ECS environment.  A PGE shall not set or redefine environmental variables.
Conflicting_FR: A PGE executed by a PROCESS_ADMIN may set or redefine environment variables only during its first execution; subsequent executions by any PROCESS_ADMIN shall be prohibited from changing environment variables.
Resolution:
1. Modify Requirement A to allow an exception for the first execution of a PGE by a PROCESS_ADMIN, aligning it with the conditions specified in Requirement B.  
2. Revise Requirement B to prohibit any PGE from setting or redefining environment variables, ensuring full compliance with Requirement A.  
3. Introduce a new requirement that explicitly defines the conditions and scope under which environment variables may be modified, reconciling the differing constraints of Requirements A and B.

Original_FR: A Program Administrator/Nursing Staff Member shall be able to add a new nursing student to the system.
Conflicting_FR: Only the System Administrator shall be permitted to add new nursing students.
Resolution:
1. Clarify and redefine roles by specifying that the Program Administrator/Nursing Staff Member can propose or request the addition of a new nursing student, but final approval and actual addition to the system is performed by the System Administrator.  
2. Update the requirements to allow both the Program Administrator/Nursing Staff Member and the System Administrator to add new nursing students, but enforce role-based permissions to track and differentiate who performed the action.  
3. Conduct a stakeholder review to determine whether the responsibility of adding new nursing students should be exclusively assigned to the System Administrator or shared with the Program Administrator/Nursing Staff Member, then revise the requirements accordingly.

Original_FR: A Program of Study shall consist of a program name and listing of required classes.
Conflicting_FR: A Program of Study may omit required classes and include elective components only.
Resolution:
1. Define separate types of Programs of Study: one that includes required classes and another that allows for elective-only components, ensuring both requirements are accommodated.  
2. Introduce a rule where Programs of Study must specify whether they include required classes, elective components, or both, with clear guidelines for each format.  
3. Revise Requirement B to clarify that elective-only Programs of Study are an exception to the standard format defined in Requirement A, ensuring consistency in program definitions.

Original_FR: A search described in requirement UCAR602 results in a displayed set of topics.
Conflicting_FR: The search described in UCAR602 shall output a summary report instead of displaying topics.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the intended user need for UCAR602 by consulting stakeholders to determine whether the output should prioritize displaying topics, generating a summary report, or offering both options.
2. Modify UCAR602 to include a user-configurable option that allows the user to choose between displaying topics or generating a summary report based on their preference.
3. Combine the outputs by displaying the set of topics alongside a summary report, ensuring both requirements are satisfied simultaneously.

Original_FR: A search described in requirement UCAR602 results in a displayed set of topics.
Conflicting_FR: A search described in requirement UCAR602 shall output a summary report instead of displaying topics.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the intended purpose of the search in UCAR602 with stakeholders to determine whether the output should prioritize displaying topics, generating a summary report, or supporting both functionalities as separate options.  
2. Modify UCAR602 to include a configuration or user preference setting that allows the search to either display topics or output a summary report based on the user's choice.  
3. Split UCAR602 into two distinct requirements: one for displaying topics and another for generating a summary report, ensuring they address different use cases or workflows.

Original_FR: A search described in requirement UCAR702 results in a displayed set of topics.
Conflicting_FR: The search described in UCAR702 shall return topic identifiers only, not full topic details.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the intended output of UCAR702 by determining whether the stakeholders need full topic details or only topic identifiers, and update both requirements to reflect the agreed-upon outcome.  
2. Modify Requirement A to specify that the displayed set of topics is derived from the topic identifiers returned by UCAR702, ensuring consistency with Requirement B.  
3. Introduce a new intermediate step where UCAR702 returns topic identifiers (per Requirement B), and a subsequent process fetches and displays full topic details if needed (to align with Requirement A).

Original_FR: A search described in requirement UCAR702 results in a displayed set of topics.
Conflicting_FR: A search described in requirement UCAR702 shall return only topic identifiers, not full topic details.
Resolution:
1. Update Requirement A to specify that the displayed set of topics consists of topic identifiers only, aligning with Requirement B.
2. Modify Requirement B to allow for the retrieval of full topic details upon user selection, while initially returning only topic identifiers.
3. Introduce a new requirement that defines a two-step process: first, displaying topic identifiers, and then providing full topic details upon user request.

Original_FR: A search described in requirements UCAR201 and UCAR202 results in a displayed set of buildings that match the search input.
Conflicting_FR: The search described in UCAR201 and UCAR202 shall return only building IDs, not the full building list.
Resolution:
1. Update Requirement A to specify that the displayed set of buildings consists of building IDs only, aligning it with Requirement B's output format.  
2. Modify Requirement B to allow the search to return both building IDs and a full building list, ensuring the display requirements in Requirement A can be met.  
3. Introduce a new requirement that enables configurable search output, allowing users to choose between displaying building IDs or the full building list based on their needs.

Original_FR: A search described in requirements UCAR201 and UCAR202 results in a displayed set of buildings that match the search input.
Conflicting_FR: A search described in requirements UCAR201 and UCAR202 shall return only building IDs, not the full building list.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the intended output of the search by consulting stakeholders to determine whether the system should display full building details, only building IDs, or provide both options based on user preferences.  
2. Modify the system to return building IDs by default but include an additional feature that allows users to request full building details after the initial search.  
3. Combine the requirements by displaying building IDs in the initial search results and providing a mechanism (e.g., a "View Details" button) to retrieve full building information as needed.

Original_FR: A search described in requirements UCAR203 results in a displayed set of POI that match the search input.
Conflicting_FR: If the quick search option is selected, the system shall use only the POI name field from the user input, ignore all other search parameters, and return the top 10 most frequently accessed POIs.
Resolution:
1. Update Requirement A to specify that the displayed set of POIs will vary depending on the search method used, including the quick search option described in Requirement B.  
2. Modify Requirement B to clarify that the quick search option overrides the standard search behavior described in Requirement A and explicitly returns results based only on POI name and frequency of access.  
3. Introduce a new requirement to define a hierarchy or priority between standard search and quick search, ensuring users are informed when quick search parameters supersede other search inputs.

Original_FR: A search described in requirements UCAR203 results in a displayed set of POIs that match the search input.
Conflicting_FR: The search described in UCAR203 shall exclude POIs from restricted areas.
Resolution:
1. Update Requirement A to explicitly state that POIs from restricted areas will be excluded from the displayed set of results to align with Requirement B.  
2. Modify Requirement B to clarify that POIs from restricted areas will not be included in the search results, ensuring consistency with the output described in Requirement A.  
3. Introduce a filtering mechanism in UCAR203 that allows users to toggle the inclusion or exclusion of restricted area POIs, providing flexibility while adhering to both requirements.

Original_FR: A search described in requirements UCAR406 and UCAR407 results in a displayed set of events that match the search input.
Conflicting_FR: The search described in UCAR406 and UCAR407 shall only return future events, not past events.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the scope of Requirement A to explicitly state whether the displayed set of events includes both past and future events or only future events, aligning it with Requirement B if necessary.  
2. Modify Requirement B to allow the user to toggle between viewing past events, future events, or both, ensuring flexibility in search results while maintaining consistency with Requirement A.  
3. Update the search functionality to include filters for past and future events, ensuring that Requirement A displays results based on user-selected criteria and Requirement B restricts default results to future events only.

Original_FR: A search described in requirements UCAR501 and UCAR502 results in a displayed set of employees that match the search input.
Conflicting_FR: Only HR administrators shall be able to perform searches on employees; regular users shall not access this function.
Resolution:
1. Update Requirement A to explicitly state that the search functionality is restricted to HR administrators, ensuring alignment with Requirement B.
2. Modify Requirement B to allow limited search access for regular users, but restrict the visibility of sensitive employee details to comply with privacy and security policies.
3. Introduce a role-based access control mechanism that enables different levels of search functionality based on user roles, with HR administrators having full access and regular users having restricted or no access.

Original_FR: A single adult shall be able to lift and carry the UAV.
Conflicting_FR: The UAV shall require at least two adults to lift and carry it safely.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the intended use case and operational context to determine whether the UAV should be designed for single-person portability or require two-person handling for safety, then update one of the requirements accordingly.  
2. Specify weight thresholds or design features (e.g., detachable components) that allow the UAV to be safely carried by a single adult but recommend two-person handling for certain scenarios.  
3. Introduce a requirement for an optional carrying aid (e.g., a cart or harness) to allow a single adult to carry the UAV while maintaining the option for two-person handling for added safety.

Original_FR: A single currency cannot occupy more than one slot.
Conflicting_FR: The CEP card may allow a currency to be assigned to multiple slots for redundancy.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the system's primary goal (e.g., strict uniqueness vs. redundancy) and update one of the requirements to align with the prioritized objective.  
2. Introduce a conditional rule where a currency can occupy multiple slots only in specific, predefined redundancy scenarios, ensuring both requirements coexist.  
3. Define separate modes of operation (e.g., "unique mode" and "redundancy mode") and allow the system to toggle between them based on user or system needs.

Original_FR: A staff member shall have the ability to manage the progress of a part-time student.
Conflicting_FR: Only academic advisors shall be permitted to modify a part-time student’s class sequence.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the roles and permissions of "staff members" and "academic advisors" to define whether "managing progress" includes modifying a student's class sequence or if it is limited to monitoring and reporting.  
2. Update Requirement A to specify that staff members can manage progress but cannot modify a part-time student’s class sequence unless they are academic advisors.  
3. Introduce a collaborative workflow where staff members can recommend class sequence changes for approval by academic advisors, ensuring compliance with Requirement B.

Original_FR: A suitably authorized CPN user (e.g. the system administrator) shall be able to install and activate NGN managed services without the intervention of the subscribing CPN user.
Conflicting_FR: New NGN managed services shall be considered approved automatically if the subscribing CPN user does not respond within 3 seconds of the one-time code being issued.
Resolution:
1. Modify Requirement A to specify that the authorized CPN user can install and activate NGN managed services only after the one-time code approval process has been completed, ensuring the subscribing CPN user's input is accounted for.  
2. Adjust Requirement B to extend the response time for the subscribing CPN user to a reasonable duration (e.g., 30 seconds), allowing sufficient time for user input before automatic approval occurs.  
3. Introduce a priority hierarchy, where actions by the authorized CPN user override the automatic approval mechanism in Requirement B, ensuring manual intervention takes precedence when necessary.

Original_FR: A suitably authorized CPN user (e.g., the system administrator) shall be able to install and activate NGN managed services.
Conflicting_FR: Only the service provider’s technical support personnel shall be allowed to install and activate NGN managed services.
Resolution:
1. Define distinct roles and permissions for CPN users and service provider technical support personnel, specifying which types of NGN managed services each role is authorized to install and activate.  
2. Establish a joint authorization mechanism requiring both a suitably authorized CPN user and the service provider’s technical support personnel to collaborate on the installation and activation of NGN managed services.  
3. Clarify the scope of responsibilities by restricting CPN users to specific administrative tasks (e.g., activation only) while reserving installation rights exclusively for the service provider’s technical support personnel.

Original_FR: A suitably authorized CPN user shall be able to manage and configure provisioning capabilities.
Conflicting_FR: Only the NGN central management system shall manage provisioning; CPN users shall not have configuration rights.
Resolution:
1. Define a tiered management system where CPN users have limited provisioning rights for specific tasks, while the NGN central management system retains full control over critical provisioning configurations.  
2. Clarify the scope of "provisioning capabilities" for CPN users in Requirement A to exclude configurations managed exclusively by the NGN central management system in Requirement B.  
3. Introduce a role-based access control mechanism allowing CPN users to request provisioning changes, subject to approval and execution by the NGN central management system.

Original_FR: A temperature setting value that is received from an individual thermostat shall be compared to the valid temperature range values.
Conflicting_FR: The system shall not perform validation against temperature range values to reduce processing overhead.
Resolution:
1. Define a configurable system mode allowing validation to be toggled on or off, so the temperature range check can be performed only when necessary.  
2. Introduce a lightweight validation mechanism that minimizes processing overhead while ensuring basic compliance with the valid temperature range.  
3. Clarify the scope of validation by specifying which scenarios require temperature range checks and exclude validation for low-priority or non-critical operations.

Original_FR: A transaction that passes merchant acquirer validations and is forwarded to the card issuer is paid for by the card issuer.
Conflicting_FR: The acquirer shall make payment to the merchant directly without card issuer validation.
Resolution:
1. Introduce a conditional process where the acquirer pays the merchant directly for low-risk transactions, while high-risk transactions require card issuer validation before payment.  
2. Redefine the payment workflow to ensure that the acquirer always pays the merchant directly, but the acquirer seeks reimbursement from the card issuer post-validation.  
3. Establish a hybrid model where the card issuer provides pre-authorization for transactions, allowing the acquirer to pay the merchant directly without waiting for additional validation.

Original_FR: A user can select the desired product from the results and proceed to the product details.
Conflicting_FR: Only registered users shall be allowed to access product details; guest users shall not.
Resolution:
1. Modify Requirement A to specify that only registered users can proceed to product details, while guest users can view the results but are prompted to register or log in to access product details.  
2. Update Requirement B to allow guest users limited access to product details, such as a preview, while requiring registration for full access.  
3. Introduce a new requirement that enforces a login prompt when a guest user attempts to access product details, ensuring alignment between both requirements.

Original_FR: A user can then select the product and view the details.
Conflicting_FR: Product detail viewing shall be available to standard users for a single 30-second preview per day; after the preview expires, standard users are blocked for the remainder of the day.
Resolution:
1. Modify Requirement A to specify that standard users can select a product and view its details only within the constraints of the 30-second daily preview outlined in Requirement B.  
2. Update Requirement B to allow standard users to view product details multiple times within the 30-second cumulative preview period, ensuring alignment with Requirement A's flexibility.  
3. Introduce a tiered user model where standard users have restricted preview access as per Requirement B, but premium users can view product details without time limitations, accommodating both scenarios.

Original_FR: A user can then select the product and view the details.
Conflicting_FR: The system shall automatically display product details without requiring explicit user selection.
Resolution:
1. Combine the requirements by allowing the system to automatically display product details for the first product in a list, while still enabling users to manually select other products to view their details.  
2. Introduce a user preference setting to toggle between automatic display of product details and manual selection, accommodating both behaviors.  
3. Define a specific context for each behavior, such as automatic display for single-product scenarios and manual selection for multi-product scenarios.

Original_FR: A user may reorder routes or remove flight plans assigned to a UAV.
Conflicting_FR: Only flight controllers shall have permission to reorder or remove UAV flight plans.
Resolution:
1. Clarify user roles by defining whether "user" in Requirement A includes flight controllers or if it refers to a broader group, and update the requirements accordingly to eliminate ambiguity.  
2. Introduce a permission hierarchy where only flight controllers can reorder or remove flight plans, but other users may request changes that require flight controller approval.  
3. Modify Requirement A to specify that users can only suggest reordering or removal of flight plans, while Requirement B retains the exclusive authority of flight controllers to execute such actions.

Original_FR: A user must be logged on to be able to view or edit any medical information.
Conflicting_FR: Users shall be able to view general medical summaries without logging in.
Resolution:
1. Modify Requirement A to specify that a user must be logged on to view or edit **detailed or sensitive medical information**, while general medical summaries are excluded from this restriction.  
2. Update Requirement B to clarify that general medical summaries available without logging in must not contain sensitive or personally identifiable medical information.  
3. Introduce a new requirement stating that general medical summaries for non-logged-in users must be anonymized or aggregated to ensure compliance with privacy and security standards.

Original_FR: Accepted input for price and destination search DESC: Integers should be accepted as input when a user searches by price or destination. If the system receives an invalid input the user should be informed and prompted to insert an accepted input.
Conflicting_FR: If the price and destination search is initiated, the THEMAS system shall accept only alphanumeric strings as input and shall reject all numerical entries, returning an error code to the calling service.
Resolution:
1. Align the input type by updating both requirements to accept alphanumeric strings for destination searches and integers for price searches, ensuring clarity on the expected behavior for each input type.  
2. Introduce separate search modes: one for price (accepting integers) and one for destination (accepting alphanumeric strings), with the system rejecting invalid inputs based on the selected mode.  
3. Consolidate the requirements to specify that the system must handle both integers and alphanumeric strings, validating inputs contextually (e.g., integers for price, strings for destination) and providing appropriate error messages for invalid entries.

Original_FR: Accepted input for price and destination search: Integers should be accepted.
Conflicting_FR: The system shall accept both integers and floating-point numbers for price and destination searches.
Resolution:
1. Update Requirement A to align with Requirement B by explicitly stating that both integers and floating-point numbers should be accepted for price and destination searches.  
2. Modify Requirement B to restrict input to integers only, ensuring consistency with Requirement A.  
3. Combine and clarify both requirements into a single unified requirement that specifies whether only integers, only floating-point numbers, or both are acceptable for price and destination searches, based on stakeholder priorities.

Original_FR: Add a new dish — Given the administrator is logged in, When the administrator creates a new dish, Then the new dish should be added.
Conflicting_FR: Only the restaurant owner shall be allowed to add a new dish, not the administrator.
Resolution:
1. Clarify roles and permissions by redefining "administrator" and "restaurant owner" to determine if they are distinct roles or if the "administrator" should inherit the permissions of the "restaurant owner" for adding dishes. Update the requirements accordingly.  
2. Restrict dish creation solely to the "restaurant owner" and remove the functionality for "administrator" to add dishes, aligning Requirement A with Requirement B.  
3. Introduce a configurable permission system where the restaurant owner can explicitly grant or revoke dish creation rights to the administrator, ensuring flexibility while resolving the conflict.

Original_FR: Add a new restaurant owner — Given the administrator is logged in...
Conflicting_FR: Only the system super-administrator shall be allowed to create new restaurant owners.
Resolution:
1. Update Requirement A to specify that only the system super-administrator, when logged in, can add a new restaurant owner, aligning it with Requirement B.  
2. Modify Requirement B to permit administrators with specific elevated permissions (besides the super-administrator) to create new restaurant owners, if this aligns with stakeholder needs.  
3. Introduce a role-based access system where the super-administrator can delegate the ability to add restaurant owners to specific administrators.

Original_FR: Add a new restaurant owner Given the administrator is logged in When the administrator creates a new restaurant owner Then the new restaurant owner should be added
Conflicting_FR: Creation of new restaurant owners shall be allowed for SUPER_ADMIN only during a single 60-second annual provisioning window; outside that 60-second window no creations are permitted.
Resolution:
1. Modify Requirement A to specify that only SUPER_ADMIN users can add new restaurant owners and ensure it aligns with the 60-second annual provisioning window described in Requirement B.  
2. Adjust Requirement B to allow administrators (in addition to SUPER_ADMIN) to create new restaurant owners, but still restrict this action to the 60-second annual provisioning window.  
3. Introduce a new requirement that permits administrators to create restaurant owners outside the 60-second provisioning window but requires approval or confirmation from a SUPER_ADMIN.

Original_FR: Add a new restaurant type — Given the administrator is logged in...
Conflicting_FR: Restaurant owners shall be allowed to define new restaurant types independently.
Resolution:
1. Define a hierarchy where administrators approve new restaurant types created by restaurant owners, ensuring compliance and consistency across the system.  
2. Allow restaurant owners to create new restaurant types independently, but notify administrators for optional review and feedback.  
3. Introduce distinct categories: administrators create global restaurant types, while restaurant owners create localized or custom types specific to their restaurants.

Original_FR: Add restaurant information — Given the administrator is logged in...
Conflicting_FR: Only the restaurant owner shall be permitted to add or modify restaurant information.
Resolution:
1. Define distinct roles for "administrator" and "restaurant owner," and clarify their respective permissions for adding or modifying restaurant information in the requirements.  
2. Update Requirement A to specify that administrators can only assist restaurant owners in adding information but cannot directly add or modify restaurant details themselves.  
3. Introduce a workflow where administrators can propose changes or assist with setup, but final approval or modifications must be completed by the restaurant owner.

Original_FR: Adding information with mandatory and optional fields — Given the restaurant owner has filled in the mandatory fields...
Conflicting_FR: The system shall reject submissions containing optional fields until verified by an administrator.
Resolution:
1. Modify Requirement B to allow submissions with optional fields but mark them as "pending verification" until an administrator reviews them, instead of outright rejecting them.  
2. Update Requirement A to specify that optional fields will only be accepted and processed after administrator verification, aligning with Requirement B.  
3. Introduce a system workflow where submissions with optional fields are flagged for review, ensuring mandatory fields are processed immediately while optional fields await administrator approval.

Original_FR: Adding information with mandatory fields — Given the restaurant owner has filled in the mandatory fields...
Conflicting_FR: Only administrators shall be permitted to add new restaurant information.
Resolution:
1. Update Requirement A to specify that restaurant owners can fill mandatory fields but submit the information only after approval by an administrator, ensuring alignment with Requirement B's permissions.  
2. Modify Requirement B to allow restaurant owners to add new restaurant information but restrict final approval and publication to administrators.  
3. Introduce a workflow where restaurant owners can submit information as drafts, which administrators review and finalize for addition to the system.

Original_FR: Adding information with mandatory fields Given the restaurant owner has filled in the mandatory fields of the form When the restaurant owner submits the form Then the information about the restaurant should be added
Conflicting_FR: New restaurant submissions shall be auto-approved if the regional manager does not review them within 2 seconds of submission; no human approval is then required.
Resolution:
1. Modify Requirement B to extend the auto-approval time window, allowing the regional manager a more reasonable timeframe (e.g., several minutes or hours) to review submissions before they are auto-approved.  
2. Introduce a validation step in Requirement A where the system verifies that all mandatory fields are correctly filled before submissions are eligible for auto-approval in Requirement B.  
3. Update Requirement B to specify that auto-approval is only applicable if mandatory field validation (from Requirement A) is successfully passed, ensuring data integrity before approval.

Original_FR: after every user request, new maps are needed to be loaded.
Conflicting_FR: The system shall cache maps and reuse them instead of loading new ones after every request.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the use case scenarios to determine when loading new maps is essential versus when caching and reusing maps is acceptable, and update the requirements accordingly.  
2. Implement a hybrid approach where maps are cached by default, but new maps are loaded only when explicitly required by specific user requests.  
3. Introduce a configuration setting that allows toggling between always loading new maps and reusing cached maps, depending on the system's operational context or user preferences.

Original_FR: After installing the Application from iTunes or the App store the user will be asked to login.
Conflicting_FR: Application launch without login shall be allowed only once per device ever; all subsequent launches shall require full authentication.
Resolution:
1. Modify Requirement A to specify that the user will be asked to log in only after the first launch, aligning it with Requirement B's condition of allowing one unauthenticated launch per device.  
2. Update Requirement B to allow the application to prompt for login immediately after installation, ensuring clarity on when the first unauthenticated launch is permitted.  
3. Introduce a new requirement specifying that the first unauthenticated launch will still prompt the user to log in but allow them to skip it, ensuring consistency between both requirements.

Original_FR: After installing the Application from iTunes or the App Store, the user will be asked to login.
Conflicting_FR: The user shall be automatically logged in after installation without a manual login step.
Resolution:
1. Modify Requirement A to allow automatic login as the default behavior, while providing an option for manual login if automatic login fails or is disabled by the user.  
2. Update Requirement B to require automatic login only if the user has previously logged in on the same device, otherwise default to a manual login step.  
3. Combine both requirements into a single hybrid approach: after installation, attempt automatic login first, and if unsuccessful, prompt the user to log in manually.

Original_FR: After selecting one of the months, the system shall generate the ASCII report file.
Conflicting_FR: The system shall generate a PDF report instead of an ASCII report file.
Resolution:
1. Update Requirement A to allow the user to select the report format (ASCII or PDF) before generating the report, accommodating both formats.  
2. Replace Requirement A with Requirement B to standardize the report format as PDF, ensuring consistency across the system.  
3. Combine both requirements by having the system generate both ASCII and PDF reports simultaneously to meet diverse needs.

Original_FR: After selecting one of the months, the system shall generate the ASCII report file.
Conflicting_FR: After selecting one of the months, the system shall generate a PDF report file.
Resolution:
1. Modify the system to generate both an ASCII report file and a PDF report file after a month is selected, ensuring compatibility with both requirements.  
2. Introduce a user preference or configuration setting that allows the user to choose between generating an ASCII report file or a PDF report file.  
3. Align stakeholders to agree on a single report format (either ASCII or PDF) based on the primary use case and system constraints, and update the requirements accordingly.

Original_FR: Agenda is restricted to what is available in the feeds on the list of agenda RSS-feeds.
Conflicting_FR: Agenda items may include externally sourced content beyond the RSS feeds.
Resolution:
1. Define a priority hierarchy where RSS feed content is mandatory for the agenda, but external content can be included as supplementary material if explicitly approved or flagged as secondary.  
2. Update Requirement A to allow exceptions for externally sourced content when RSS feeds do not cover specific agenda needs, ensuring these exceptions are clearly documented.  
3. Introduce a configurable toggle that enables users to choose whether the agenda strictly adheres to RSS feeds or allows the inclusion of external content.

Original_FR: All information must be updatable on the website.
Conflicting_FR: Only administrators shall be able to update website information; general users shall not.
Resolution:
1. Clarify Requirement A to specify that "information must be updatable on the website by authorized users," aligning it with the administrator-only update restriction in Requirement B.  
2. Update Requirement B to explicitly state that administrators are the sole authorized users for updating website information, ensuring consistency with Requirement A's intent.  
3. Introduce a role-based access control mechanism to allow administrators to update all information while enabling general users to suggest updates or edit specific non-critical content, subject to administrator approval.

Original_FR: All information must be viewable on the website.
Conflicting_FR: Certain sensitive information shall not be viewable on the website.
Resolution:
1. Define specific categories of sensitive information that must be excluded from website visibility and explicitly list exceptions within Requirement A.  
2. Implement role-based access controls on the website to ensure sensitive information is only viewable to authorized users.  
3. Modify Requirement A to state that "All non-sensitive information must be viewable on the website," aligning it with Requirement B.

Original_FR: All letter requests must be formatted according to guidelines specified by the Print Letter Utility system.
Conflicting_FR: Letter requests may follow either the system format or user-defined templates.
Resolution:
1. Define a priority hierarchy where user-defined templates are allowed only if they meet minimum compatibility criteria with the Print Letter Utility system's formatting guidelines.  
2. Update Requirement A to explicitly allow exceptions for user-defined templates, provided they are pre-approved or validated by the system.  
3. Introduce a system feature to convert user-defined templates into the required format, ensuring consistency with the Print Letter Utility guidelines.

Original_FR: All on-line messages must have the ability to include card issuer discretionary data from the CEP card.
Conflicting_FR: Online messages shall exclude issuer discretionary data for security reasons.
Resolution:
1. Define a configurable option that allows issuer discretionary data to be included or excluded in online messages based on the specific use case or security context.  
2. Allow issuer discretionary data to be included in online messages but ensure it is encrypted or masked to address security concerns.  
3. Conduct a risk assessment to determine scenarios where issuer discretionary data is critical, and revise the requirements to allow its inclusion only in those specific cases while maintaining security controls.

Original_FR: All Pilot communication with the UAV shall require secure connections.
Conflicting_FR: Pilots may use unsecured connections for training flights.
Resolution:
1. Update Requirement B to specify that unsecured connections for training flights are only permitted when operating in a controlled and isolated environment, ensuring no risk to UAV security or operations.  
2. Introduce a new requirement mandating that unsecured connections for training flights must be monitored and logged to detect any potential vulnerabilities or misuse.  
3. Modify Requirement A to allow exceptions for training flights, provided a risk assessment is conducted and security measures are implemented to mitigate potential threats.

Original_FR: All registered customer information shall be stored and maintained in the database even after account cancellation.
Conflicting_FR: Customer data shall be permanently deleted once the account is canceled.
Resolution:
1. Define a retention policy: Specify a time period (e.g., 90 days) during which customer data is retained after account cancellation, after which it is permanently deleted. This balances data maintenance and deletion requirements.  
2. Introduce a data categorization approach: Retain essential non-personal data (e.g., transaction history) for business purposes while permanently deleting personal identifiable information (PII) upon account cancellation.  
3. Allow customer consent: Provide customers with the option to choose whether their data should be retained or permanently deleted upon account cancellation, ensuring compliance with data privacy preferences.

Original_FR: All searchable product-specific metadata in MODIS standard products shall be formatted as specified in DID 311.
Conflicting_FR: MODIS metadata may follow updated format guidelines replacing DID 311.
Resolution:
1. Update Requirement A to explicitly allow adherence to updated format guidelines by stating: "All searchable product-specific metadata in MODIS standard products shall be formatted as specified in DID 311 or its officially approved updates."
2. Revise Requirement B to clarify the conditions for adopting updated guidelines, such as requiring formal approval or compatibility checks with existing systems.
3. Introduce a governance process to evaluate and approve updates to metadata format guidelines, ensuring alignment between DID 311 and any future updates.

Original_FR: All streaming movie sales will be logged in the database and accessible by the administrator.
Conflicting_FR: Sales logs shall be accessible to both administrators and authorized auditors.
Resolution:
1. Update Requirement A to explicitly include authorized auditors as part of the access group, aligning it with Requirement B (e.g., "All streaming movie sales will be logged in the database and accessible by the administrator and authorized auditors").  
2. Revise Requirement B to restrict access to administrators only, matching Requirement A, or clarify that auditors' access is conditional and requires additional permissions.  
3. Define a shared access policy in a new requirement that specifies distinct roles and access levels for administrators and auditors, ensuring consistent terminology and scope.

Original_FR: All streaming movie sales will be logged in the database and accessible by the administrator.
Conflicting_FR: Streaming movie sales logs shall be viewable by admins for a single continuous 10-second interval each month; for all other times only internal audit may view them.
Resolution:
1. Modify Requirement A to specify that administrators can access streaming movie sales logs only during the 10-second interval outlined in Requirement B, aligning both requirements with the same access limitation.  
2. Adjust Requirement B to allow administrators continuous access to streaming movie sales logs, while still granting internal audit exclusive access outside of the administrator's usage period.  
3. Introduce a tiered access policy where administrators can access aggregated or anonymized sales data at all times (Requirement A), but full detailed logs are restricted to the 10-second interval and internal audit (Requirement B).

Original_FR: All UAV data transmission shall be secure against unauthorized access.
Conflicting_FR: UAV data may be transmitted in plaintext during test operations.
Resolution:
1. Update Requirement B to specify that plaintext transmission during test operations is only permissible when the data is non-sensitive and explicitly marked for testing purposes, ensuring alignment with Requirement A's security mandate.  
2. Introduce a conditional clause in Requirement A stating that secure transmission is mandatory except during authorized test operations where plaintext transmission is explicitly approved and monitored.  
3. Implement encryption during test operations with an option to disable it temporarily for specific non-critical test scenarios, ensuring plaintext transmission is logged and reviewed for compliance.

Original_FR: All UAV data transmission shall be secure from unauthorized access.
Conflicting_FR: UAV data may be broadcast without encryption for telemetry testing.
Resolution:
1. Define a specific operational mode for telemetry testing where encryption is intentionally disabled, ensuring this mode is restricted to authorized personnel and controlled environments.  
2. Require encryption for all UAV data transmissions and implement a secure mechanism to provide authorized testers with decryption keys for telemetry testing.  
3. Establish a policy mandating that unencrypted telemetry testing is only permitted with non-sensitive or simulated data to mitigate security risks.

Original_FR: All users shall be able to zoom into images using standard gestures.
Conflicting_FR: Only premium users shall be able to zoom into images.
Resolution:
1. Clarify user roles and privileges by defining a tiered zoom functionality where all users can zoom to a basic level, but premium users get enhanced zoom features (e.g., higher zoom levels or advanced controls).  
2. Update Requirement B to specify that only premium users can zoom into certain types of images (e.g., high-resolution or exclusive content), while all users can zoom into standard images.  
3. Align both requirements by introducing a configurable setting that allows the zoom feature to be toggled based on the organization's business strategy, enabling flexibility in access for different user tiers.

Original_FR: All users should be able to go back to the product category page via the back button.
Conflicting_FR: Only registered users shall have access to the back navigation feature.
Resolution:
1. Update Requirement A to specify that the back button feature is available to all users except in cases where access restrictions apply, aligning it with Requirement B.  
2. Revise Requirement B to clarify that registered users have enhanced back navigation features, while unregistered users have limited or alternative navigation options.  
3. Introduce a conditional design where the back button behavior adapts based on user status (e.g., registered users access full navigation, while unregistered users are redirected to a default page).

Original_FR: All users should be able to navigate using the sub-navigation icons to access PDFs, videos, and contacts.
Conflicting_FR: Only authenticated users shall access the catalogue PDF and 360 videos.
Resolution:
1. Modify Requirement A to specify that unauthenticated users can access only general navigation and public content, while access to restricted items like the catalogue PDF and 360 videos requires authentication.  
2. Update Requirement B to clarify that authentication is required only for specific restricted content, ensuring that general navigation and access to public resources remain available to all users.  
3. Introduce a new requirement explicitly distinguishing between public and restricted content, defining access levels for each user type (authenticated vs. unauthenticated).

Original_FR: All users should be able to search by keyword or product ID.
Conflicting_FR: Only employees shall be able to perform keyword or product ID searches.
Resolution:
1. Clarify user roles and permissions by defining which specific user groups (e.g., regular users vs. employees) are allowed to perform searches, ensuring Requirement B aligns with the organization's security policy.
2. Implement a tiered access system where keyword and product ID searches are available to all users but with limited results for regular users, while employees receive full access to detailed search results.
3. Conduct stakeholder interviews to determine the business rationale behind each requirement, then adjust one or both requirements to align with the organization's strategic goals and user needs.

Original_FR: All Users will be able to view the next product by swiping left to right .
Conflicting_FR: The THEMAS system shall display the next product only after the user double-taps the product image.
Resolution:
1. Align the navigation method by choosing a single interaction (either swipe or double-tap) based on user research and usability testing, ensuring consistency across the system.  
2. Allow both interactions (swipe and double-tap) for navigating to the next product, providing flexibility to accommodate diverse user preferences.  
3. Differentiate the interactions: use swiping for browsing through products and double-tapping for additional actions, such as selecting or focusing on a product.

Original_FR: All users will be able to view the next product by swiping left to right.
Conflicting_FR: The next product shall be displayed automatically without requiring a swipe action.
Resolution:
1. Redefine the behavior based on user preference by introducing a setting that allows users to choose between automatic display or swipe-based navigation for the next product.  
2. Combine both behaviors by enabling automatic display of the next product after a brief delay, but also allow users to manually swipe left to right to skip the delay.  
3. Conduct user research to determine the preferred interaction method and update both requirements to align with the majority preference.

Original_FR: Application should do the distance calculations and geolocation information for user/site and nearby sites.
Conflicting_FR: If the distance calculation feature is invoked, the system shall require the user to manually enter the latitude and longitude coordinates for both the origin and destination points.
Resolution:
1. Modify Requirement A to specify that distance calculations and geolocation information will only be automatic when available, but will require manual input of coordinates if geolocation data is not accessible.  
2. Update Requirement B to allow the system to automatically retrieve latitude and longitude coordinates using geolocation services, with manual input as a fallback option.  
3. Introduce a new requirement specifying a configuration setting where users can choose between automatic geolocation or manual input for distance calculations, based on their preferences or system capabilities.

Original_FR: At the start of each turn the product shall notify each player of his or her status.
Conflicting_FR: The Game Master account shall be permitted to view/announce player status only during the first 5 seconds after a turn starts; outside that window no viewing/announcing is allowed.
Resolution:
1. Modify Requirement A to specify that the product shall notify players of their status only within the first 5 seconds of a turn to align with Requirement B's constraints.  
2. Adjust Requirement B to expand the time window for the Game Master to view/announce player status to match the duration of the notification process in Requirement A.  
3. Introduce a new mechanism where the product notifies players of their status independently without requiring Game Master involvement, ensuring both requirements are met without conflict.

Original_FR: Both IMS capable devices with or without UICC and non-IMS capable devices without UICC shall be supported.
Conflicting_FR: IMS service access shall be permitted to devices with an active UICC only for a single TCP session lasting no longer than 3 seconds; any longer sessions shall be terminated.
Resolution:
1. Modify Requirement A to explicitly state that IMS service access is restricted to devices with an active UICC, aligning it with the conditions outlined in Requirement B.  
2. Revise Requirement B to allow IMS service access for devices without a UICC, ensuring compatibility with the broader support mandate in Requirement A.  
3. Introduce a new requirement specifying distinct service access rules for IMS-capable devices with and without UICC, clearly delineating their respective capabilities and limitations.

Original_FR: Build Ajax Response to update home page with a link to nearby site based on user location
Conflicting_FR: When the user location is not available, the system shall display a default list of popular sites and update the home page with the generated Ajax Response.
Resolution:
1. Modify Requirement A to include a fallback mechanism, specifying that if the user location is unavailable, the system should default to displaying a list of popular sites as described in Requirement B.  
2. Clarify Requirement B to explicitly state that the default list of popular sites should only be used when the user location is unavailable, ensuring it complements, rather than conflicts with, Requirement A.  
3. Combine both requirements into a unified requirement that outlines the priority: first attempt to use the user's location to generate a link to a nearby site, and if unavailable, display the default list of popular sites.

Original_FR: Build the search engine based on the search form and sites available
Conflicting_FR: When the search form is submitted, the system shall ignore any user-input keywords and instead execute a pre-defined search for "breaking news" across all available sites.
Resolution:
1. Modify Requirement B to allow user-input keywords to take precedence, but provide an optional "breaking news" quick search button that executes the pre-defined search across all sites.  
2. Update Requirement A to specify that the search engine must support both user-input keyword searches and pre-defined searches like "breaking news," with clear prioritization rules.  
3. Introduce a system configuration option that allows administrators to toggle between user-input keyword searches (Requirement A) and pre-defined searches (Requirement B) based on the desired use case.

Original_FR: CNDs connected to the CPN and devoted to specific managed services shall be remotely manageable
Conflicting_FR: Full remote management privileges granted to CND-MGMT-ADMIN shall be limited to a single 30-second session per day; attempts outside that session are denied.
Resolution:
1. Define a tiered access system: Allow CND-MGMT-ADMIN full remote management privileges as specified in Requirement B, but introduce additional user roles with extended remote management privileges to meet the operational needs of Requirement A.
